title: BFS to Find Connected Components
description: Algorithm to find number of connected components using Breadth-First Search
code:
PROCEDURE FindComponents(G)
 n ← |V(G)|
 visited[1..n] ← false
 components ← 0

 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 IF NOT visited[i] THEN
 components ← components + 1
 BFS(i)
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN components
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE BFS(start)
 queue ← empty
 visited[start] ← true
 Enqueue(queue, start)

 WHILE queue NOT empty DO
 v ← Dequeue(queue)
 FOR each neighbor u of v DO
 IF NOT visited[u] THEN
 visited[u] ← true
 Enqueue(queue, u)
 END IF
 END FOR
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Start from any unvisited vertex, perform BFS to mark all reachable vertices as visited (same component). Increment component count and repeat for any remaining unvisited vertices.

title: Check if Graph is Connected
description: Algorithm to determine if a graph is connected
code:
PROCEDURE IsConnected(G)
 n ← |V(G)|
 visited[1..n] ← false

 BFS(1) // Start from first vertex

 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 IF NOT visited[i] THEN
 RETURN false
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN true
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Perform BFS from any vertex (here vertex 1). If after BFS all vertices are visited, graph is connected; otherwise disconnected.

title: DFS to Find Components
description: Depth-First Search based algorithm for component detection
code:
PROCEDURE DFSComponents(G)
 n ← |V(G)|
 visited[1..n] ← false
 componentID[1..n] ← 0
 compCount ← 0

 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 IF NOT visited[i] THEN
 compCount ← compCount + 1
 DFS(i, compCount)
 END IF
 END FOR

 RETURN compCount
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE DFS(v, cid)
 visited[v] ← true
 componentID[v] ← cid

 FOR each neighbor u of v DO
 IF NOT visited[u] THEN
 DFS(u, cid)
 END IF
 END FOR
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Recursive DFS marks all vertices in current component with same componentID. New component started when unvisited vertex found.
